title: Removing Contiguous Duplicates from Rotated Array

statement.markdown:
## Problem Description

Given an array of integers rotated an unknown number of times, remove all contiguous duplicates while preserving the original order. For example, if the array is `[2, 2, 3, 4, 4, 4, 5, 5, 5, 5]`, remove the duplicates to get `[2, 3, 4, 5]`.
       ## Constraints
       - The array may contain duplicates that are not contiguous.
       - The array may have a small size or large size.
       - It is acceptable to modify the original array.
       - Return an array with no duplicates.
       ## Example Input/Output
       - Input: `nums = [2, 2, 3, 4, 4, 4, 5, 5, 5, 5]`
       - Output: `nums = [2, 3, 4, 5]`
statement.inputFormat: An array of integers representing the rotated array.
statement.outputFormat: The modified array with no contiguous duplicates.

examples.explanation: Remove the duplicates in the input array

hints.text: First hint text: Identify and remove the first occurrence of each duplicate.

editorial.title: Removing Contiguous Duplicates from Rotated Array
editorial.intuition: Use two pointers to traverse the array and keep track of duplicates.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force algorithm: Use a HashMap to store unique elements.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool removeDuplicates(int a[], int n) { for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) { if(i > 0 && a[i] == a[i - 1]) continue; return true; } return false; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean removeDuplicates(int[] nums) { for(int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { if(i > 0 && nums[i] == nums[i - 1]) continue; return true; } return false; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution( object ): def removeDuplicates( self, nums ): for i in range( len( nums ) ): if i > 0 and nums[ i ] == nums[ i - 1 ]: continue return True return False
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function removeDuplicates( a ) { for( var i = 0; i < a.length; i++ ) { if( i > 0 && a[ i ] == a[ i - 1 ] ) continue; return true; } return false; }

solutions.python: class Solution( object ): def removeDuplicates( self, nums ): n = len( nums ) count = 0 for i in range( n ): if i == 0 or nums[ i ] != nums[ i - 1 ]: nums[ count ] = nums[ i ] count += 1 return count print( ' '.join( map( str, nums ) ) )
solutions.cpp: #include <iostream> using namespace std; class Solution { public: int removeDuplicates(int a[], int n) { int count = 0; for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) { if(i == 0 || a[i] != a[i - 1]) a[count++] = a[i]; } return count; } }; int main() { Solution *s = new Solution(); int a[] = {2, 2, 3, 4, 4, 4, 5, 5, 5, 5}; int n = sizeof(a)/sizeof(a[0]); s->removeDuplicates(a, n); for(int i = 0; i < n; i++) cout << a[i] << ' '; cout << endl; return 0; }
